items.q: How much does solar installation cost?
items.a: 3kW costs ₹1.6–2.15 lakh before subsidy. After PM Surya Ghar ₹78,000 subsidy, net cost is ₹82,000–1.07 lakh. EMI from ₹300/month. 1kW: ₹30K subsidy | 2kW: ₹60K | 3kW+: ₹78K.

items.q: How to apply for PM Surya Ghar in Gujarat?
items.a: Register at pmsuryaghar.gov.in with your DISCOM consumer number. Skylar India handles all paperwork completely free. Subsidy credited within 30 days of installation.

items.q: How many units does a 3kW system generate daily?
items.a: Gujarat has 300+ sunny days. A 3kW system generates 12–15 units/day (360–450/month), saving ₹2,500–3,500/month or ₹30,000–42,000/year.

items.q: When does my solar investment pay back?
items.a: On-grid with subsidy: 3–5 year payback. Off-grid with battery: 5–7 years. Then 20+ years of free electricity. Total 25-year savings: ₹5–10 lakh+.

items.q: What solar pump subsidy is available for farmers?
items.a: PM-KUSUM: Central 30% + State 30% = 60% on solar pumps (3–10HP). Small/marginal farmers get up to 90% subsidy. Annual savings vs diesel: ₹50,000–1,00,000+.
Panel cleaning: ₹500–1,000/year. Inverter check: ₹1,000–2,000. Skylar India AMC packages available. Panels maintain 80%+ efficiency for 25 years.

items.q: Does solar work on cloudy or rainy days?
items.a: Cloudy days reduce output 10–25% but Gujarat's 300+ annual sunny days ensure excellent annual output. On-grid uses grid backup; hybrid has battery. Net metering covers rainy periods.
No! Waterproof sealants & professional mountings used. Compatible with RCC, metal & terracotta roofs. 1–3 day install with zero damage guarantee.
